items.final_wording: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.

source.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with symptoms or signs that may indicate cancer, subject to recommendation-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Primary care recognition, safety netting, investigation, and referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected suspicious skin-lesion, safety-netting, and age- or risk-qualified respiratory recommendations; international suspected-cancer pathways require UAE clinician review. This batch also reviews recommendation-qualified upper-GI dysphagia, jaundice, and colorectal rectal-bleeding fields; no cancer diagnosis or automatic referral text is generated. This batch additionally reviews recommendation-qualified head-and-neck features; no cancer diagnosis or automatic referral text is generated. This batch also reviews the breast-cancer recognition section for clinician-assessed breast lumps and qualified nipple or skin changes; generic breast pain is not treated as equivalent evidence. source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician recognition of rectal bleeding, change in bowel habit, abdominal pain, weight loss, anaemia, and rectal-mass contexts with recommendation-specific age qualifiers; it does not assert cancer.

items.rationale: The exact colorectal section supports symptom, age, rectal-bleeding, bowel-habit, weight-loss, anaemia, and existing FIT context without interpreting the result or assigning referral urgency.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.
